mission.title: Install and Configure Kuma on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kuma is a modern Envoy-based service mesh that simplifies service connectivity, discovery, security, and observability across Kubernetes and VMs. Installing Kuma allows you to manage complex microservices architectures with ease, leveraging its multi-zone capabilities.

steps.title: Add the Kuma Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Kuma Helm repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add kuma https://kumahq.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kuma using Helm
steps.description:
Install Kuma with the following command, creating a namespace called 'kuma':
```bash
helm install kuma kuma/kuma --version v2.13.2 --namespace kuma --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Kuma pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kuma
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Kuma
steps.description:
Update the Kuma deployment to set resource limits. Edit the deployment with:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kuma-control-plane --namespace kuma
``` 
Then add the following under 'spec.template.spec.containers':
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for the Kuma control plane
steps.description:
Forward the Kuma control plane port to access the dashboard:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/kuma-control-plane 5681:5681 --namespace kuma
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Kuma control plane running in the 'kuma' namespace, with all pods in the 'Running' state. You can access the Kuma dashboard via port forwarding.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kuma https://kumahq.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install kuma kuma/kuma --version v2.13.2 --namespace kuma --create-namespace

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy Kuma.
